info.name: IDFC Integration - manual test (SFDC / digital / sync)
info.description:
Importable into Postman AND Insomnia. Requests use {{variables}} (Variables tab) so you can retarget hosts/tokens in one place. IDs use Postman's {{$guid}} so each send is unique; in Insomnia replace {{$guid}} or use its uuid tag. SERVICE ports are 8080/8081/8082 (the 191xx remap is for the vendor MOCKS the services call, not for you); kafka-ui is 8085.

Folders 1-4 hit the edges over HTTP. Folder 5 covers engine journeys that have NO edge HTTP door (device-validation brand-variety, vehicle-rc, employee-lwd): they are triggered by producing a CanonicalEnvelope to a consumed topic, which folder 5 does via the kafka-ui produce API (each request also carries the guaranteed docker-exec kafka-console-producer fallback).

NOT included by design: negative-area-verification, domain-check-verification, emandate-cancel and emandate-autopay-setup. Their journey files exist but in the default local (classpath) run they are neither loaded (not in the engine's local journey-resources) nor routable (no type-to-journey row), so they cannot run out of the box. To exercise them, publish them via the DAG Designer/journey-registry and add a type-to-journey row, then produce an envelope like folder 5.

Watch results: engine ops runs (:8082) / Sync Invocations tab / kafka-ui :8085 (DLQ orig.sfdc.dlq.v1). Prereqs: infra + services up (docker compose -f docker-compose.infra.yml up -d, then run-services.ps1).

request.description: Routes edge->orig.sfdc.pl.v1, engine->loan-origination. Expect an async run COMPLETED_APPROVED.
negativeFlags=[FRAUD] -> COMPLETED_DECLINED (a business no, not a failure).
Different svcName -> orig.device-validation.v1 -> device-validation journey. Apple = block-only config.
Inbound_Wrapper maps to loan-origination in the engine (scaffold). Routes to a run.
svcName -> comm.sms.send.v1 -> communications capability (fire-and-forget). No journey run; check communications.log. Verify the exact Mobile__c/Description fields against the capability.

CORRECTION: LAP is NOT a DLQ case. The edge routes LAP -> orig.sfdc.lap.v1 and the engine's base type-to-journey maps LAP -> loan-origination, so this runs the SAME journey as PERSONAL_LOAN but via a different topic (proves business-line -> topic routing). A genuinely unmapped type -> engine DLQ is the Kafka-door request in folder 5, because every edge svcName is engine-mapped.
Edge routes BUSINESS_LOAN -> orig.sfdc.bl.v1; engine maps BUSINESS_LOAN -> loan-origination. Same journey, another business-line topic.
Edge routes COMMERCIAL -> orig.sfdc.commercial.v1; engine maps COMMERCIAL -> loan-origination. Completes the PL/LAP/BL/Commercial routing set.
OrganizationId not in known-orgs -> refresh+recheck -> DLQ. Tests the org config allow-list.
No edge routing row for FOO_BAR -> fail closed at the edge. Tests svcName routing discipline.
Async multi-partner origination. 200 ACK_PROCESSED, then loan-origination run COMPLETED_APPROVED.
negativeFlags=[FRAUD] -> outcome REJECTED / COMPLETED_DECLINED.
type=FOO has no route -> 422 UNROUTABLE.
GET the outcome. REPLACE the last path segment with the applicationId from the origination response (e.g. DIG-GROWW-APP-...). PENDING -> APPROVED.
status S + transactionId. Shows in the Sync Invocations tab as SUCCESS. (Re-send with the SAME idempotentId to see a deduped replay.)
custBankAccNo=BAD-ACCOUNT -> status F -> BUSINESS_FAILURE (teal), still HTTP 200.
custBankAccNo=SERVER-ERROR -> vendor 500 -> edge 502 -> TECHNICAL_ERROR (red).
Offer with LOAN_AMOUNT/ROI -> SUCCESS.
crnNo=NO-OFFER-CRN -> empty resource_data -> SUCCESS (a query returning none is not a failure).
requestCode not in known-request-codes -> 422 UNKNOWN_REQUEST_CODE (backend never called) -> TECHNICAL_ERROR.
The sync-lane audit list (imps/lms). Fail-closed without both headers.
The journey runs list on the engine. Watch the SFDC/digital journeys land here.

item.name: 5. Engine journeys via Kafka UI (no HTTP edge door)
